mission.title: Install and Configure Kubefleet on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeFleet is an open-source Kubernetes multi-cluster application management solution that simplifies governance and workload management across multiple clusters. Installing KubeFleet allows you to efficiently manage applications in a hybrid or multi-cloud environment.

steps.title: Set up clusters for testing
steps.description:
KubeFleet uses a hub-spoke model. Create clusters:
```bash
kind create cluster --name hub
kind create cluster --name member1
```

steps.title: Install KubeFleet on the hub cluster
steps.description:
Clone the repo and install from local charts:
```bash
kubectl config use-context kind-hub
git clone https://github.com/kubefleet-dev/kubefleet.git
cd kubefleet
helm install hub-agent charts/hub-agent --namespace kubefleet-system --create-namespace
```
See https://kubefleet-dev.github.io/website/docs/getting-started/kind/ for the full quickstart.

steps.title: Install member agent on member clusters
steps.description:
On each member cluster:
```bash
kubectl config use-context kind-member1
helm install member-agent charts/member-agent --namespace kubefleet-system --create-namespace \
  --set hubCluster.apiServer=<hub-api-server-url>
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl config use-context kind-hub
kubectl get pods -n kubefleet-system
kubectl get memberclusters
```

resolution.summary: KubeFleet is installed on the hub cluster with member clusters joined for multi-cluster workload management.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeFleet
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall hub-agent -n kubefleet-system
helm uninstall member-agent -n kubefleet-system
kind delete cluster --name hub
kind delete cluster --name member1
```

upgrade.title: Pull latest and upgrade
upgrade.description:
```bash
cd kubefleet && git pull
helm upgrade hub-agent charts/hub-agent --namespace kubefleet-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Member cluster not joining
troubleshooting.description:
Check the member agent logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=member-agent -n kubefleet-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Hub controller not starting
troubleshooting.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubefleet-system
kubectl logs -l app=hub-agent -n kubefleet-system
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your cluster.
